features: The Skoda Kushaq facelift will be launched in India tomorrow. 20 Mar 2026
The Skoda Kushaq facelift prices are set to be announced on 21 March. 17 Mar 2026
The Skoda Kushaq is powered by a 1.0-litre three-cylinder TSI engine that makes 114bhp and 178Nm of torque.
The eight-speed torque converter (TC) also shifts smoothly and accelerates fast, making city traffic easy to manage.
The Sports and Manual driving modes and paddle shifters further improve the fun and control.
The braking performance was also strong, progressive and consistent throughout our testing phase.
It has an ARAI-claimed mileage of 19.09kmpl and a range of up to 955km.
The wind noise is muted very well, but road and engine noise seep into the cabin.
Ride quality at low speeds feels firm.
This powertrain is mated to a seven-speed DCT (dubbed DSG by Skoda) transmission.
It gets electric sunroof as standard, while the higher trims get a large panoramic sunroof.
It will get 491-litres of boot space, expandible to 1400-litres with rear seats folded.
The Skoda Kushaq facelift has scored five-stars in Global NCAP crash safety tests.
Up close at the front, the grille in the top-spec Monte Carlo variant also features red accents.

features: Tata Nexon SUV has topped the sales chart in February with 19,430 unit sales. 5 Mar 2026
ADAS is available on the Tata Nexon Fearless+ PS DCT variant. 16 Oct 2025
It scored five stars in BNCAP crash safety tests and gets six airbags and ISOFIX anchors as standard.
It gets modern features such as a panoramic sunroof, JBL sound system, and wireless charger.
The new touch-based AC controls look great, respond well, and include handy toggle switches.
Thanks to its 209mm ground clearance and balanced suspension, it handles potholes confidently.
Fit and finish feels subpar in places and design oddities such as bottle holder and cubby placement could’ve improved.
The touchscreen infotainment display seems to lag or freeze at times.
There are drive modes – Eco, City and Sport, with the engine being the most responsive in Sport mode.
The strong mid-range power delivery post 2,000rpm and the ample torque ensure it does not require frequent gear shifts.
The clutch pedal has reasonable weight and the gears engage properly via the lever.
It has adequate grunt both at city crawling speeds and out on the highway to be a proper all-rounder.
The steering has a good amount of heft and the active response helps the Nexon change directions quickly.
The brakes are good, there’s ample grip, hardly leans through corners, and is a great car to cover long distances.
Whichever drive mode you use, it takes a deep breath before moving ahead.
It is mated to a six-speed manual gearbox with eco, city and sport driving modes.
It has an electric sunroof that can be operated with voice commands and an air purifier.
It is offered with a 10.25-inch floating infotainment system by Harman and eight JBL speakers including a subwoofer.
Although supportive, the seat cushioning on both rows feels slightly hard to be seated on.
The horn button placement is too far from one’s thumb to reach without letting go of the steering.
Nexon Price in Mumbai
The Kylaq has a strong road presence and carries styling cues that make it look like a mini Kushaq.
With 17-inch alloys and 189mm ground clearance, it’s well-prepared for Indian road conditions.
The 446-litre boot is practical, and can be further increased to a massive 1,265 litres by folding the rear seats.
Seating comfort is a highlight, especially at the rear where the seatback angle and cushioning feel supportive.
The rear seat is best suited for two average-sized adults, making it less ideal for three occupants.
It doesn't feature modern staple features like ADAS and a 360-degree camera.
In terms of transmission, it gets a six-speed manual or six-speed torque converter (TC) automatic gearbox options.
The direct injection and turbocharging make the Kylaq feel punchy and smooth during gearshifts.
This FWD compact SUV has good power delivery right from the beginning, with strong low-end performance.

The suspension tuning delivers a plush, absorbent ride, keeping passengers comfortable.
The cabin layout and supportive seats make long journeys comfortable, suitable for five adults.
The hybrid system helps boost the fuel efficiency and cuts fuel costs in a meaningful way.
The engine offers ample power, well-suited for both city commutes and highway cruising.
The hybrid system’s battery packaging eats into boot space, limiting luggage capacity.
On rougher roads, the suspension can feel firm, quite uneasy for the occupants in the cabin.
Material quality in the cabin lacks the premium feel expected in this segment.
The headroom in the second row is quite limited due to the battery placed underneath.
The hybrid Urban Cruiser Hyryder pulls smoothly from a standstill, thanks to the EV mode.
It returned 17.7kmpl in the city and an impressive 27.38kmpl on the highway in our fuel efficiency tests.
Although the driving experience of this hybrid variant is effortless, it is far from being exciting or engaging.
The ride quality is harsh over broken roads and it does not feel settled when driving over wavy road surfaces.
A panoramic sunroof along with the ambient lighting feature elevates the Hyryder’s cabin ambience.
The SmartPlaycast touchscreen with Android Auto and Apple CarPlay compatibility offers connectivity.
There are ventilated front seats with artificial leather upholstery which enhance comfort in varying climates.
There is a 360-degree camera combined with the rear parking sensors to simplify parking and manoeuvring in tight spaces.
The Toyota Urban Cruiser Hyryder has not yet received a crash-test rating from the NCAP authorities.
It lacks adaptive cruise control and other advanced ADAS functions that competitors offer at this price point.
The ambient lighting, soft-touch panels, and premium stitched upholstery enhance cabin aesthetics.

This engine seems to struggle when you need some punch; feels adequate for maybe a Polo, but not an SUV like the Taigun.

insights.title: New Major Competitor in Auto Market
insights.description: The detection of Tata Motors Cars as a new competitor signals an active expansion or increased visibility of a major, established automotive player in our competitive landscape. This matters because new significant entrants can disrupt existing market share, pricing structures, and customer expectations, potentially challenging our current market positioning and growth targets.
insights.action: Initiate an immediate competitive deep-dive into Tata Motors' current vehicle portfolio, pricing strategies, brand perception, and recent market performance to assess the immediate threat and identify potential vulnerabilities.

source_traces.snippet: New competitor detected: Tata Motors Cars - Sedans, Hatchbacks, SUVs | Tata Motors Limited

insights.title: Broad Segment Entry by New Competitor
insights.description: Tata Motors' explicit mention of Sedans, Hatchbacks, and SUVs indicates a broad-spectrum approach targeting key consumer vehicle segments. This comprehensive entry into multiple core segments suggests a direct competitive threat across our product lines, potentially leading to increased competition for customer attention and sales volume in these crucial areas.
insights.action: Conduct a detailed product-to-product comparison, benchmarking our Sedans, Hatchbacks, and SUVs against Tata Motors' offerings in terms of features, pricing, and target demographics to identify areas of direct overlap and potential differentiation opportunities.

source_traces.snippet: New competitor detected: Tata Motors Cars - Sedans, Hatchbacks, SUVs | Tata Motors Limited

insights.title: Established Conglomerate's Renewed Focus
insights.description: The emergence of 'Tata Motors Limited' as a new competitor underscores that this is a strategic move by a large, well-resourced conglomerate, not a nascent startup. This matters because an established player brings significant financial backing, R&D capabilities, and an existing distribution network, suggesting a serious, long-term commitment to capturing market share that could exert sustained pressure on pricing and innovation.
insights.action: Analyze Tata Motors' corporate strategic announcements, investor relations reports, and recent technology investments (e.g., EVs, autonomous driving) to anticipate their long-term competitive strategy and potential disruptive innovations.

source_traces.snippet: New competitor detected: Tata Motors Cars - Sedans, Hatchbacks, SUVs | Tata Motors Limited

whitespace.description: No competitor offers detailed, comparable feature lists or clear explanations of their 'best-in-class' claims (e.g., specific safety technologies, performance metrics, or unique tech innovations). Customers struggle to understand the tangible value and differentiate between models without this information.
whitespace.suggested_action: Create a comprehensive 'Features & Technology' section for each vehicle model, including interactive breakdowns of safety systems, engine specifications, infotainment features, and unique selling points. Implement a comparison tool allowing users to directly compare specific features across multiple models.
whitespace.supporting_evidence: The `features` array for the competitor is empty. The `meta_description` mentions 'best-in-class safety, performance and technology' but provides no specifics, indicating a lack of detailed feature exposition.

whitespace.description: The competitor only lists 'starting prices' and 'ex-showroom prices', but there's a significant lack of transparency around financing options, EMI calculators, leasing programs, or estimated total cost of ownership (e.g., insurance, maintenance packages). This leaves customers in the dark about the true affordability and long-term financial commitment.
whitespace.suggested_action: Integrate an interactive EMI calculator on product pages, partner with financial institutions to offer pre-approved loan options, and clearly outline different leasing plans. Offer transparent maintenance and service packages that detail long-term ownership costs.
whitespace.supporting_evidence: The `pricing` data only includes 'Price starts at' and 'Introductory ex-showroom price' for various models. There is no mention of financing, EMI, leasing, or total cost of ownership in the provided data.

whitespace.description: There is a complete absence of social proof such as customer testimonials, reviews, or case studies. In a high-consideration purchase like a car, potential buyers heavily rely on the experiences and endorsements of existing customers to build trust and validate their decision.
whitespace.suggested_action: Implement a dedicated 'Customer Stories' or 'Testimonials' section on the website, featuring written reviews, video testimonials, and user-generated content from satisfied car owners. Encourage and showcase customer ratings for specific models.
whitespace.supporting_evidence: The `testimonials` array in the competitor data is empty, indicating no presence of customer reviews or endorsements.

whitespace.description: No competitor offers a personalized vehicle recommendation engine or an advanced online car configurator. Customers currently have to browse broad categories (SUVs, sedans) without tools that help them filter based on their specific lifestyle, budget, or feature preferences, leading to a less efficient and engaging discovery process.
whitespace.suggested_action: Develop an interactive quiz or 'Find Your Perfect Car' tool that asks users about their needs (e.g., family size, usage, budget, desired features) and recommends suitable models. Build an advanced online configurator allowing users to customize trim levels, colors, accessories, and view real-time price updates.
whitespace.supporting_evidence: The competitor's `ctas` include 'Learn more' and 'Book Now,' but there are no CTAs or descriptions indicating a 'configure your car,' 'build your own,' or 'get recommendations' feature.

overused_angles.whitespace_hint: AI is ubiquitous; the whitespace is in 'explainable AI', human-in-the-loop control, or AI-free guarantees for compliance-sensitive markets.
